understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to win each level by getting every goal tile marked with a '+' occupied by a selectable piece at the same time (if there are multiple '+' tiles, all must be covered simultaneously). Completing a level when the engine reports WIN progresses toward finishing the full KA59 run.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: There is at least one selectable piece; actions operate on the currently selected piece (the log always referred to a 'Selected piece'). CLICK attempts to move that selected piece to the clicked grid coordinate: when the destination was valid/unblocked (e.g., clicking [9, 21] or [18, 21]), the piece relocated exactly to that coordinate; when the destination was blocked or otherwise invalid (e.g., [37, 19], [36, 18], [35, 17]), the piece did not move. Obstacles/walls prevent movement/placement into certain tiles. The level is step-limited, and actions consume the remaining steps.

orient_history: Movement acts in 3-pixel (1 cell) increments; some pieces may be immobile due to surrounding walls, so we may need to switch selection to the other selectable and try moving it toward the goal area.

CLICK relocates the currently selected selectable to the clicked (grid-aligned) empty cell; MOVE attempts stepwise movement but is blocked by collisions. To win, we likely need to place a selectable onto the goal region.

CLICK likely attempts to reposition/teleport the currently-selected piece to the clicked location (but only if the destination is a valid empty/goal tile and the click is within the tile bounds). Previous CLICK at [33,15] may have missed the valid goal hitbox (clicked on an edge/corner).

CLICK selects a selectable piece at the target position; MOVE_* attempts to slide the currently selected 3x3 piece by one cell (3 pixels). We must get selectables onto the goal (+) area; if one piece is fully surrounded by walls, we should switch to the other piece and move it instead.

Hypothesis: CLICK on a selectable changes which piece is selected (the 'selected marker' moves), while MOVE_* attempts to move the selected piece by one cell (3 px) but is blocked by walls. To make progress, we must find a selectable that can move toward the goal or can be positioned onto it.

Hypothesis: CLICK selects a piece at the target position; MOVE_* attempts to slide the currently selected piece by 1 cell (3 pixels) but is blocked by collidable walls/geometry. To make progress, we must switch to the other selectable and see if it has any legal moves toward the goal.

Hypothesis: CLICK on a selectable switches which piece is selected (the log reports this as the 'selected piece moved'). Only the selected piece can be moved with MOVE_*. Different selectables may have different freedom of movement depending on nearby walls.

CLICK on a selectable switches the active/selected piece; MOVE_* moves the selected piece by 1 cell (3 pixels) but walls block movement. Since the current selected piece is immobile, we should switch to the other selectable and test its mobility to reach the goal area.

Hypothesis: CLICK on a selectable switches selection; CLICK elsewhere attempts to teleport/move the selected piece to the clicked position if valid/reachable, otherwise nothing happens. Since the goal is the only target region, clicking its exact pixel origin may be required to trigger a valid placement/teleport interaction.

Hypothesis: CLICK selects a selectable (possibly swapping which is controlled). Movement feasibility depends on which selectable is selected; need to find one with an escape route to reach the goal region.

Hypothesis: CLICK selects/interacts with objects at the clicked pixel. MOVE_* attempts to move the currently selected 1x1-cell piece but is blocked by collidable walls. There may be an interactable wall/door (e.g., wall_24_12) that can be toggled/removed via CLICK to open a path to the goal.

Hypothesis: CLICK attempts to move/teleport the selected piece to the clicked location if the click lands within a valid destination region (possibly needs to be inside the goal object's bounding box, not on its edges). Previous goal clicks may have missed the active hitbox by landing on boundaries. If we can place the selected piece onto the goal region by clicking the goal center, we can then try to place the other selectable as well.

CLICKing on the other selectable appears to swap the selected piece’s position with that selectable (works even through walls). Movement options may differ depending on which piece/position is currently selected, so we should test the other position for available MOVE_* exits.

CLICK movement likely requires grid-aligned (multiples of 3) pixel coordinates for the 3x3 piece; the goal’s stored position [35,17] is not grid-aligned, so clicking it didn’t register as a valid placement. Goal region in semantic grid corresponds to cell (11,5) => pixel [33,15], which should be a valid aligned target inside the + area.

Hypothesis: movement is grid-locked by walls; CLICK on another selectable acts like a swap/teleport between the two selectable positions, letting us try actions from the other pocket (which may have a path or further interaction toward the goal).

CLICK likely changes which selectable is currently controlled/selected (previous logs showing 'moved' were actually selection switching). Arrow moves apply only to the currently selected piece, so we should select the other piece to check if it has any available movement/openings.

CLICK likely teleports/moves the selected selectable to the clicked empty/valid tile if that tile is not a wall; prior failed goal clicks may have been on non-snapped pixels or non-valid goal cells. We should CLICK a clearly valid goal cell aligned to the 3-pixel grid (multiples of 3).

CLICK on a selectable likely changes which piece is selected (the log reports it as the 'selected piece moved' because selection switches to the clicked piece). To make progress, we should check whether the other selectable has different mobility or can be moved toward the goal.

Hypothesis: MOVE_* moves the currently selected piece by one cell but only if adjacent space is free; CLICK on a selectable swaps selection (and may swap the two pieces’ positions), while clicking walls/goal/empty has no effect. If one piece has any mobility, we must select it and navigate it to the goal chamber.

Hypothesis: MOVE actions are impossible due to walls; CLICK can either (a) teleport the selected piece to another selectable when clicking it, or (b) attempt to teleport/move the selected piece to the clicked destination if it is a valid target (e.g., inside the goal). Previous failed clicks likely targeted wall/invalid tiles rather than the actual goal hitbox.

Hypothesis: CLICK on a selectable switches the active piece (even though the log reports it as a ‘move’). Movement may only be possible from the other selectable if it has an opening; we should switch selection to inspect its available moves.

CLICK on a selectable likely changes the selected piece (the log reports it as the selected piece 'moving' to that location). Movement (MOVE_*) only affects the currently selected piece, so we should switch selection to see if the other piece has an open move toward the goal.

Hypothesis: CLICK may support placing/teleporting the currently selected piece onto certain objects if the click lands correctly (possibly needing to click inside the goal’s true pixel bounds/center, since the goal object is offset and not aligned to the 3-pixel cell grid). If this works, we can win despite being boxed in by walls.

Hypothesis: arrow moves attempt to shift the selected piece by 3px (1 cell) but can be blocked by adjacent walls. CLICKing another selectable swaps/teleports the selected piece to that selectable's position. To reach the goal, we may need to operate from the other selectable position to see if it has any available movement or interaction options.

CLICK likely changes which selectable is active when clicking on a selectable; direct CLICK-to-teleport onto the goal seems to fail when attempted with the currently selected piece, so next test is selecting the other piece and retrying goal placement.

Hypothesis: MOVE_* attempts to slide the currently selected selectable by 3 pixels (1 cell) but can be blocked by walls; CLICK on another selectable switches control to that piece (reported as 'moved' because the 'player' entity changes). To reach the goal, we likely must select the piece that has a path to move.

CLICK likely changes the selected controllable piece when clicking on a selectable. Movement onto goals must be done with MOVE_* actions, so we need to find a selectable that is not fully surrounded by walls and can reach the goal area.

CLICK likely attempts to place/teleport the selected piece to the exact pixel coordinate clicked, and success may require snapping to the 3-pixel grid (multiples of 3) inside the goal area. The goal object’s top-left ([35,17]) is off-grid, so clicking it may fail; we should click an aligned cell coordinate within the '+' area (e.g., [36,18]).

Hypothesis: MOVE_* attempts to move the currently selected selectable by one cell (3 pixels) and is blocked by collidable walls. CLICK only works to select another selectable (changing the active 'player'); clicking non-selectable tiles (including goal) has no effect. To make progress, we must select the other piece and see if it has any legal moves toward the goal area.

Hypothesis: CLICK on another selectable swaps which piece is currently active (reported as the 'selected piece moved'), while MOVE_* is blocked due to enclosed pockets. There may be a special-case where a CLICK into the goal area can teleport the currently-selected piece onto the goal, but it might depend on which selectable is active or exact targeting.

CLICK appears to attempt to move/teleport the currently selected piece to the clicked pixel position only if that destination is valid (not blocked/invalid). MOVE_* moves are grid-step attempts but currently all are blocked by surrounding walls. Since we cannot move normally, the only remaining chance is that clicking the exact goal anchor point might be treated differently than clicking nearby inside the goal area.

Rules inferred: CLICK teleports/moves the currently selected 3x3 selectable to the clicked cell position if not blocked; WIN requires all goal tiles (+) to be simultaneously covered by selectables. With 0 steps remaining, the engine will not apply additional moves/clicks, so we cannot reach the goal now.

Agent explained goal and mechanics.

understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to win each level by getting every goal tile marked with a '+' occupied by a selectable piece at the same time (if there are multiple '+' tiles, all must be covered simultaneously). Completing a level when the engine reports WIN progresses toward finishing the full KA59 run.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: There is at least one selectable piece; actions operate on the currently selected piece (the log always referred to a 'Selected piece'). CLICK attempts to move that selected piece to the clicked grid coordinate: when the destination was valid/unblocked (e.g., clicking [9, 21] or [18, 21]), the piece relocated exactly to that coordinate; when the destination was blocked or otherwise invalid (e.g., [37, 19], [36, 18], [35, 17]), the piece did not move. Obstacles/walls prevent movement/placement into certain tiles. The level is step-limited, and actions consume the remaining steps.
